Description: Key equation, chemical or physical, evaluated in this step.
Attrition Scrubbing: N/A (mechanical). However, inputs to this step are: crushed claystone, recycled coarse materials, recycled water, and makeup water. Assuming a target of ~20 wt% solids, a grain density of 2.6 t/m3 and water density of 1 t/m3. r_mix = 1 / (w_s / rho_s + (1 - w_s) / (rho_water)) --> conversion factor is 1/(w_s*r_mix). Need to multiply by density of input ore; assuming 20% of this comes from the recycling in step 3, we have a conversion factor of ≈10.41
Acid Leaching (at ~33 wt% solids after a: Acid leaching of Li-bearing clays (simplified): Li₂O·nSiO₂·mH₂O (s) + H₂SO₄ (aq) → Li₂SO₄ (aq) + nSiO₂ (s) + (m+1)H₂O. Other gangue elements (Al, Mg, Ca, Fe) form soluble sulfates or secondary solids.
Primary CCD / Leach Residue Thickening (: N/A (multi-stage CCD solid–liquid separation and washing; no net chemistry beyond minor neutralization). Not present in Thacker Pass but in TLC, Bonnie Claire, previous Thacker pass disclosures.
Impurity Removal and Neutralization: Impurity precipitation / neutralization, e.g. Fe³⁺ + 3OH⁻ → Fe(OH)₃(s); Mg²⁺ + CO₃²⁻ → MgCO₃(s); Ca²⁺ + CO₃²⁻ → CaCO₃(s); Al³⁺ + 3OH⁻ → Al(OH)₃(s). Lithium remains mostly in solution.

Crushing: Primary & secondary crushing, generates crushed ore for wet circuit
Classification: Hydrocyclones+screens to split coarse vs fine; assume coarse refeed to achieve input/output; fines feed thickener; output conversion factor tied to input of step 2 - see note in conversion factor there.
Thickening (to ~55 wt% solids leach feed: Concentration of slurry, recovery of process water. From step 2, ~8.773 vol% are solids. With the corresponding densities, ~22.81 t are solid and ~91.23 t are liquid per 100 m3 (20 wt% solid). If ~22.81 t is to be ~55 wt%, then the total weight is 41.47 t --> water reduced from 91.23 t to 18.66 t ≈ 72.57 t to overflow; 41.47 t of 55wt% is 8.773+18.66 m3 ≈ 27.433 m3, thus this conversion factor of 0.27
Acid Leaching (at ~33 wt% solids after a: Heated or ambient acid leach in agitated tanks or pachuca-type reactors; converts Li-bearing minerals to soluble Li sulfate solution while leaving gangue largely solid; output is leach slurry (solution + residue). 22.81 t/h of solids --> 69.12 t/h total at 33%, or 46.31 t/h water. The previous mix had 22.81 t/h solids and 18.66 t/h water, so we'd need roughly 27.76 t/h of water to be added. Volumetrically, that previous mix had a volume of 27.43 m3/h. The new leach slurry volume is 55.08 m3/h, so the conversion factor would be 27.43/55.08 ≈ 0.498 ≈ 0.5.
Primary CCD / Leach Residue Thickening (: Counter-current decantation (CCD) of leach slurry to separate pregnant Li solution from residual solids and wash entrained Li from residue; produces pregnant leach solution (PLS) and leach residue to tailings.
Impurity Removal and Neutralization: Chemical removal of impurities (Mg, Ca, Fe, Al, etc.) via pH adjustment and precipitation; produces purified Li solution for downstream recovery and an impurity sludge for disposal.
